model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: St. John's is one of the strongest 5-seeds in the field, ranking 18th-20th nationally with an adjusted efficiency margin of +24.8, nearly 13 points higher than Northern Iowa's +11.86. The Red Storm enter on a dominant run, winning all three Big East Tournament games by double digits including a 72-52 rout of UConn in the championship, and their elite defense (AdjD 95.1, 12th nationally) should stifle Northern Iowa's below-average offense (AdjO 110.1, 151st nationally). While UNI has tremendous momentum from their Cinderella Arch Madness run as the lowest seed ever to win the MVC Tournament, the significant talent and efficiency gap—combined with Rick Pitino's tournament experience—makes this a matchup where the higher seed should prevail comfortably despite the historically upset-prone 5-12 line.

reasoningStep.title: East 5/12: St. John's vs Northern Iowa
reasoningStep.summary: St. John's holds a commanding advantage in efficiency metrics, defensive quality, and overall resume, making them a strong favorite despite the historically upset-prone 5-12 seed line.
evidence: St. John's ranks 18th (Torvik) and 20th (KenPom) with a +24.8 AdjEM vs Northern Iowa's 70th/71st ranking and +11.86 AdjEM
St. John's defense ranks 12th nationally (AdjD 94.8-95.1) and should contain UNI's 151st-ranked offense (AdjO 110.1)
St. John's won the Big East Tournament as the 1-seed, beating UConn 72-52 in the final, winning all three games by double digits
Northern Iowa won the MVC Tournament as the lowest seed ever, winning 4 games in 4 days to end a 10-year NCAA drought on a 5-game winning streak
The ~13-point efficiency margin gap is unusually large for a 5-12 matchup, suggesting St. John's is underseeded relative to their quality

arguments.query: "5 vs 12" upset rate NCAA tournament history 5 seed 12 seed

modelTrace.content: Excellent! Now I have great context. Let me get a bit more detail on Northern Iowa's tournament run and St. John's momentum.

I now have sufficient data to make my analysis. Let me compile everything:

**St. John's (5 seed)**:
- Record: 27-6
- KenPom: #20 (AdjO 119.9, AdjD 95.1, AdjEM +24.8)
- Torvik: #18 (AdjO 119.4, AdjD 94.8)
- Conference: Big East (1 seed, won Big East Tournament beating UConn 72-52 in final)
- Won all 3 Big East tourney games by double digits
- Elite defense (#12 Torvik AdjDE)
- Strong offense (#46-47)
- Coached by Rick Pitino

**Northern Iowa (12 seed)**:
- Record: 23-12
- KenPom: #71 (AdjO 110.1, AdjD 98.2, AdjEM +11.86)
- Torvik: #70 (AdjO 109.7, AdjD 98.5)
- Conference: MVC (won Arch Madness as lowest seed ever, 4 wins in 4 days)
- On 5-game winning streak
- Ending 10-year NCAA drought
- Led by senior guard Trey Campbell
- Defense is solid (#26 Torvik AdjDE)
- Offense is below average (#151-153)

**Key differentials**:
- AdjEM gap: +24.8 vs +11.86 = ~13 point gap (very large)
- St. John's offense (119.9) vs UNI defense (98.2) = strong advantage for SJU
- UNI offense (110.1) vs SJU defense (95.1) = SJU defense should contain UNI
- 5-12 upsets happen ~35% historically, but this is a strong 5 seed

St. John's is playing at an elite level, riding momentum from a dominant Big East Tournament run, and has a massive efficiency margin advantage. While UNI has momentum from their Cinderella MVC run, the talent and quality gap is substantial. The 5-12 upset is always a consideration, but St. John's profile is more like a 3-4 seed than a typical 5.
